xml.parsers.expat/xmlparser/buffer_size.name: buffer_size
xml.parsers.expat/xmlparser/buffer_size.description: The size of the buffer used when buffer_text is true. A new buffer size can be set by assigning a new integer value to this attribute. When the size is changed, the buffer will be flushed.

info.excerpt: The size of the buffer used when buffer_text is true. A new buffer size can be set by assigning a new integer value to this attribute. When the size is changed, the buffer will be flushed.

xml.parsers.expat/xmlparser/buffer_text.name: buffer_text
xml.parsers.expat/xmlparser/buffer_text.description: Setting this to true causes the xmlparser object to buffer textual content returned by Expat to avoid multiple calls to the CharacterDataHandler() callback whenever possible. This can improve performance substantially since Expat normally breaks character data into chunks at every line ending. This attribute is false by default, and may be changed at any time. Note that when it is false, data that does not contain newlines may be chunked too.

info.excerpt: Setting this to true causes the xmlparser object to buffer textual content returned by Expat to avoid multiple calls to the CharacterDataHandler() callback whenever possible. This can improve performa...

xml.parsers.expat/xmlparser/buffer_used.name: buffer_used
xml.parsers.expat/xmlparser/buffer_used.description: If buffer_text is enabled, the number of bytes stored in the buffer. These bytes represent UTF-8 encoded text. This attribute has no meaningful interpretation when buffer_text is false.

info.excerpt: If buffer_text is enabled, the number of bytes stored in the buffer. These bytes represent UTF-8 encoded text. This attribute has no meaningful interpretation when buffer_text is false.
